dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.1 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

iCloud Sync now keeps Claude Code OAuth credentials local. Account roster, MCP connector tokens (Slack / ClickUp / Google / GitLab), and claude.ai web cookies still roam between Macs, but each new Mac runs its own `claude /login` per account — so a stolen iCloud bundle can never authenticate as you. Multi-Mac merge is additive. Connecting Slack on Mac A and ClickUp on Mac B no longer drops one of the tokens when their pushes interleave; both connectors survive. Shared connectors merge the same way. Removed the Daily Briefing feature entirely. Settings → Briefing tab, the standalone Briefing window, Daily Plan / Command modes, the news-feed scheduler, and the Carbon ⌥X hotkey are gone. Daily window is chat-only now.

Existing iCloud bundles written by older Macs may still carry Claude OAuth blobs. The next push from this Mac overwrites the bundle with the new metadata-only shape — give it one auto-sync cycle (~6h) or click Sync now to flush immediately. Account removal is not propagated yet. If you remove an account on Mac A while Mac B still has it, Mac B's next push will resurrect the account in the bundle. Use the Disconnect button on each Mac if you want it gone everywhere.
